rulings.Case Title: VINCE MORECI VS. FULTON STREET CONSTRUCTION INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Disqualification Of All Counsel Of Record For Defendant Scaffold Solutions Inc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 11, 2020, Line 1, INTERVENOR STARSTONE NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Disqualification Of All Counsel Of Record For Defendant Scaffold Solutions Inc Plaintiff-in-intervention Starstone National Insurance Company's motion for disqualification of all counsel of record for defendant Scaffold Solutions Inc. is denied. Intervenor lacks standing to bring this motion. In California state court, the party seeking disqualification must have or have had an attorney-client relationship with the "target" attorney. (See Marriage of Murchison (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 847, 851; see Great Lakes Construction, Inc. v. Burman (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 1347, 1356 [a "standing" requirement is implicit in disqualification motions].) Intervenor does not show it had or has an attorney-client relationship with Scaffold Solution's counsel. Intervenor argues that as a non-client with a personal stake in the motion it does have standing to bring the motion pursuant to Colyer v. Smith (C.D. Cal. 1999) 50 F.Supp.2d 966. Colyer's minority view of standing is not binding on this court. (Great Lakes Construction, Inc.,186 Cal.App.4th at 1351.) However, even applying the reasoning in Colyer, Intervenor does not satisfy the two elements. (See Colyer, 50 F.Supp.2d at 973 [must show an "invasion of a legally protected interest which is (a) concrete and particularized, and (b) actual or imminent, not conjectural or hypothetical"].) Intervenor argues that disqualification is proper based on the court's obligation to ensure the fair administration of justice; however, this is not enough to give a non-client standing. (See Colyer, 50 F.Supp.2d at 973 [broad interest in the administration of justice is insufficiently concrete and particularized to support a finding of standing].) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for Scaffold Solutions, Inc. is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: LAURINE JONOPOLUS VS. AT&T ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 11, 2020, Line 3, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint: Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted. AT&T has not shown that it would be prejudiced by allowing the City to file the cross-complaint, nor that the City has unduly delayed in seeking leave. It would further judicial economy and would be in the interest of justice for all related issues to be resolved in the same action. (Code Civ. Proc. § 428.50.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: BRENDAN MANNIX VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Brendan Mannixs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Pursuant To Pitchess
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 11, 2020, Line 2, Plaintiff Brendan Mannixs' Motion To Compel Discovery Pursuant To Pitchess. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is unopposed and granted. The Court will undertake a Pitchess review of the police officer's personnel file. The hard copy file shall be delivered to the Court by hand delivery to 1015 Irwin Street, San Rafael, CA 94901 within twenty days of the date of service of a copy of the Order with Notice of Entry. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 11, 2020, Line 6, PLAINTIFF RONALD CLARK's Motion To Quash Subpoena; For A Protective Order; And For An Award Of Fees And Costs. The court will hear oral argument directed to defendant's need for specific document categories. The court proposes a plaintiff counsel first-look at the employment records. If plaintiff determines a subpoenaed record should be redacted the court will undertake in camera review of the redacted documents in unredacted form to confirm redaction is appropriate. Both parties' request for sanctions will be denied. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN GUSTAVO FLORES ET AL VS. AMERICAN TECHNOLOGIES, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Approval Of Paga Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 11, 2020, Line 6, PLAINTIFFs JUAN FLORES and JOSE SOLIS LUGO's Motion For Approval Of Paga Settlement: Plaintiffs' motion for approval of PAGA settlement is granted. The Court finds that the overall amount of the proposed settlement is fair and adequate in view of the purposes and policies of PAGA, taking into account such factors as the adequacy of the civil penalties in light of the potential maximum if the matter were to go to verdict and the risks of litigation. (Lab. Code § 2699(l)(2); see Haralson v. U.S. Aviation Services Corp. (N.D. Cal. 2019) 383 F.Supp.3d 959, 972; Flores v. Starwood Hotels & Resorts Worldwide, Inc. (C.D. Cal. 2017) 253 F.Supp.3d 1074, 1077; O'Connor v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2016) 201 F.Supp.3d 1110, 1132-35.) The Court also finds that the proposed award of $74,250 in attorneys' fees, which represents 33% of the gross settlement amount of $225,000, and actual costs and expenses incurred not to exceed $15,000, is a reasonable amount in the circumstances of the case, including the result achieved, contingent risk, and counsel's lodestar. (See Laffitte v. Robert Half Int'l Inc. (2016) 1 Cal.5th 480.) Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing. =(302/EPS)
